perturbation.original_text: You will make reasonable and appropriate efforts to safeguard Evaluation Material from disclosure by you or your Representatives to anyone other than as permitted hereby by using, in all material respects, the same degree of care that you use to protect your own confidential, proprietary information.
perturbation.changed_text: You will make reasonable efforts, but not necessarily appropriate, to safeguard Evaluation Material from disclosure by you or your Representatives to anyone other than as permitted hereby by using, in all material respects, a lesser degree of care than you use to protect your own confidential, proprietary information.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the obligation to make 'reasonable and appropriate' efforts to just 'reasonable', and changing to use 'a lesser degree of care' than proprietary information, it contradicts the implied standard of care in other sections, reducing legal enforceability. This contrasts sharply with the implicit requirement for high confidentiality elsewhere. By using less care there is an In-Text contradiction on what the company is asking.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You will make reasonable efforts, but not necessarily appropriate, to safeguard Evaluation Material from disclosure by you or your Representatives to anyone other than as permitted hereby by using, in all material respects, a lesser degree of care than you use to protect your own confidential, proprietary information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the standard of care required for safeguarding Evaluation Material. The original text requires 'reasonable and appropriate efforts' using 'the same degree of care' as protecting proprietary information, implying a high standard. The changed text lowers this to 'reasonable efforts, but not necessarily appropriate' and 'a lesser degree of care', directly contradicting the expectation of high confidentiality and creating an in-text contradiction as the company is now explicitly asking for less care to be taken.

perturbation.original_text: The term “Representatives” means, as to any person, such person’s affiliates (only to the extent that they receive Evaluation Material) and its and their respective directors, officers, employees, managing members, general partners, advisors, agents or representatives (including legal advisors) and, subject to paragraph 5 below, financing sources, but only to the extent that such advisors, agents, representatives or financing sources receive Evaluation Material.
perturbation.changed_text: The term “Representatives” means, as to any person, such person’s affiliates (only to the extent that they receive Evaluation Material) and its and their respective directors, officers, employees, managing members, general partners, advisors, agents or representatives (excluding legal advisors) and, subject to paragraph 5 below, financing sources, but only to the extent that such advisors, agents, representatives or financing sources receive Evaluation Material.
perturbation.explanation: Excluding legal advisors from the definition of 'Representatives' creates a contradiction with paragraph 1, where it states 'Evaluation Material (or portions thereof) may be disclosed to those of your Representatives who need to know such information solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction'. By not including legal advisors, it is not clear who can be included, or what portions can or cannot be disclosed, since the advisors will not be able to get legal advise as they are not included.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You and your Representatives will (i) use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction with the Company involving you and (ii) keep the Evaluation Material strictly confidential and (except as required by applicable law, regulation or legal process, and only after compliance with paragraph 6 below under the heading “Compelled Disclosure”) will not, without the Company’s prior written consent, disclose any Evaluation Material to any person, except that the Evaluation Material (or portions thereof) may be disclosed to those of your Representatives who need to know such information solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction (it being understood that prior to such disclosure your Representatives will be informed of the confidential nature of the Evaluation Material and directed that such information is to be kept confidential and not used for any purpose other than the evaluation of the possible Transaction).
perturbation.justification: The changed text excludes 'legal advisors' from the definition of 'Representatives'. The contradicted text states that 'Evaluation Material (or portions thereof) may be disclosed to those of your Representatives who need to know such information solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction'. By explicitly excluding legal advisors, the change creates a contradiction because it's no longer clear whether legal advisors can receive Evaluation Material for the purpose of evaluating a possible transaction, which introduces ambiguity and conflicts with the original intent of who can receive the information.

perturbation.original_text: You agree to be responsible for any failure by your Representatives to comply with this Agreement (including, without limitation, any actions or inactions by your Representatives that would constitute a breach if such Representatives were original signatories hereto (other than with respect to paragraph 7 below under the heading “Non-Solicitation and Non-Hire of Employees”)).
perturbation.changed_text: You agree to be responsible for any failure by your Representatives to comply with this Agreement, but only for failures related to disclosure of financial information. All other failures are the sole responsibility of the individual Representative.(including, without limitation, any actions or inactions by your Representatives that would constitute a breach if such Representatives were original signatories hereto (other than with respect to paragraph 7 below under the heading “Non-Solicitation and Non-Hire of Employees”)).
perturbation.explanation: By limiting the responsibility to 'failures related to disclosure of financial information', it introduces a contradiction, as the agreement holds the recipient responsible for all Representatives' failures. This creates uncertainty about liability for other breaches, such as misuse of evaluation material. It implies that failures by representatives of the company are not held to the failure of disclosure of financial information
perturbation.contradicted_text: You and your Representatives will (i) use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction with the Company involving you and (ii) keep the Evaluation Material strictly confidential and (except as required by applicable law, regulation or legal process, and only after compliance with paragraph 6 below under the heading “Compelled Disclosure”) will not, without the Company’s prior written consent, disclose any Evaluation Material to any person, except that the Evaluation Material (or portions thereof) may be disclosed to those of your Representatives who need to know such information solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction (it being understood that prior to such disclosure your Representatives will be informed of the confidential nature of the Evaluation Material and directed that such information is to be kept confidential and not used for any purpose other than the evaluation of the possible Transaction).
perturbation.justification: The original text holds the recipient responsible for *any* failure of their representatives to comply with the agreement. The changed text limits that responsibility to failures related only to 'disclosure of financial information.' This creates a direct contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text,' which requires representatives to keep the Evaluation Material strictly confidential and only use it for evaluating a possible transaction. The changed text implies that if a representative misuses the Evaluation Material, the recipient is not responsible, directly conflicting with the original agreement's broader responsibility and thus causing an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, (i) you and your Representatives will not be required to delete, erase or destroy any Evaluation Material contained in an archived computer backup system stored as a result of automated back-up procedures (it being agreed that you and your Representatives will not access such archived computer files containing any such Evaluation Material after such delivery or destruction is otherwise required) and (ii) you and/or your Representatives may retain one copy of the Evaluation Material (and may only access any such Evaluation Material) to the extent and for so long as such retention and access by you or such Representative, as applicable, is required by law or regulation; provided , however , that in the case of each of clauses (i) and (ii) of this sentence, notwithstanding any other provision of this Agreement, you and your applicable Representatives will continue to be bound by the terms of this Agreement as if it were in full force and effect with respect to any such Evaluation Material for so long as you or your applicable Representatives retain any such Evaluation Material.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, (i) you and your Representatives will be required to delete, erase or destroy any Evaluation Material contained in an archived computer backup system stored as a result of automated back-up procedures (it being agreed that you and your Representatives will not access such archived computer files containing any such Evaluation Material after such delivery or destruction is otherwise required) and (ii) you and/or your Representatives may retain one copy of the Evaluation Material (and may only access any such Evaluation Material) to the extent and for so long as such retention and access by you or such Representative, as applicable, is required by law or regulation; provided , however , that in the case of each of clauses (i) and (ii) of this sentence, notwithstanding any other provision of this Agreement, you and your applicable Representatives will continue to be bound by the terms of this Agreement as if it were in full force and effect with respect to any such Evaluation Material for so long as you or your applicable Representatives retain any such Evaluation Material.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a direct contradiction within paragraph 10 of the agreement. Initially, it states that Evaluation Material in archived backups does not need to be deleted but must not be accessed. The modified text then states this Evaluation Material must be deleted. This creates an In-Text Contradiction, where it has to be deleted and not deleted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If you determine that you do not wish to proceed with a Transaction or your evaluation hereof, you will promptly advise the Company of that decision. In that case, or if at any time the Company so requests in writing, you will promptly (and in any event within 10 days) either, at your option, (i) deliver to the Company all of the Evaluation Material (including all copies, reproductions, summaries, analyses or extracts thereof or based thereon) in your possession or in the possession of any of your Representatives or (ii) destroy or cause to be destroyed all such Evaluation Material in your possession or in the possession of any of your Representatives (such destruction to be acknowledged by you in writing to the Company). Notwithstanding such delivery or June 20, 2016 Page 5 destruction of the Evaluation Material, you agree that you and your Representatives will continue to be bound by your obligations under this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, (i) you and your Representatives will be required to delete, erase or destroy any Evaluation Material contained in an archived computer backup system stored as a result of automated back-up procedures (it being agreed that you and your Representatives will not access such archived computer files containing any such Evaluation Material after such delivery or destruction is otherwise required) and (ii) you and/or your Representatives may retain one copy of the Evaluation Material (and may only access any such Evaluation Material) to the extent and for so long as such retention and access by you or such Representative, as applicable, is required by law or regulation; provided , however , that in the case of each of clauses (i) and (ii) of this sentence, notwithstanding any other provision of this Agreement, you and your applicable Representatives will continue to be bound by the terms of this Agreement as if it were in full force and effect with respect to any such Evaluation Material for so long as you or your applicable Representatives retain any such Evaluation Material.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that Evaluation Material in archived backups does not need to be deleted but must not be accessed. The changed text then states this Evaluation Material must be deleted. This creates a direct contradiction within paragraph 10 of the agreement (which is also the contradicted text), where it has to be deleted and not deleted.

perturbation.original_text: You acknowledge that money damages and remedies at law may be inadequate to protect the Company against any actual or threatened breach of or failure to comply with this Agreement by you or by your Representatives and, without prejudice to any rights and remedies otherwise available to the Company, you may agree to the seeking of specific performance, injunctive relief and other equitable remedies in the Company’s favor, and you further agree to waive, and to cause your Representatives to waive, any requirement for the securing or posting of any bond in connection with any such remedy.
perturbation.changed_text: You acknowledge that money damages and remedies at law may be adequate to protect the Company against any actual or threatened breach of or failure to comply with this Agreement by you or by your Representatives and, without prejudice to any rights and remedies otherwise available to the Company, you may agree to the seeking of specific performance, injunctive relief and other equitable remedies in the Company’s favor, and you further agree to waive, and to cause your Representatives to waive, any requirement for the securing or posting of any bond in connection with any such remedy.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'inadequate' to 'adequate' directly contradicts the assertion that monetary damages may be insufficient, creating a conflict about the available legal remedies. This creates uncertainty as the adequate has to be proven, leading to conflict with other parts of the agreement. It creates an In-Text contradiction due to the the legal remidies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You acknowledge that money damages and remedies at law may be adequate to protect the Company against any actual or threatened breach of or failure to comply with this Agreement by you or by your Representatives and, without prejudice to any rights and remedies otherwise available to the Company, you may agree to the seeking of specific performance, injunctive relief and other equitable remedies in the Company’s favor, and you further agree to waive, and to cause your Representatives to waive, any requirement for the securing or posting of any bond in connection with any such remedy.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that money damages may be 'inadequate,' while the changed text asserts they may be 'adequate.' The contradicted text repeats the changed text, which is a contradiction with the original text, leading to a strong in-text contradiction, which causes uncertainty regarding the appropriateness of legal remedies and conflicts with the initial agreement's intent.
